title: CPU-Bound Processing
concept: Because string splitting and dictionary lookup happen on the CPU, tokenization speed depends on thread count and string length, not GPU size.

exercises.prompt: Which hardware component is responsible for cutting text into tokens and looking them up in a dictionary?
exercises.explain: Tokenization involves reading text, splitting words, and looking them up in a index table. This requires general logical processing, which is handled by the CPU.

cards.title: GPU Idle, CPU Sweating
cards.body: Did you know that having the most expensive graphics card in the world won't make your text preparation run a single second faster? Before the AI can dream up answers, your CPU has to do the heavy lifting of cutting up the text!

cards.title: What is CPU-Bound?
cards.body: CPU-Bound Processing means a task is limited by the speed of your computer's main processor (CPU). Tokenization (chopping text into pieces and looking them up in a dictionary) is a classic CPU-bound task because GPUs are designed for heavy math, not for organizing and splitting text.

cards.title: The Chef and the Oven
cards.body: Imagine chopping a pile of carrots. The GPU is like a giant, super-fast oven that cooks dishes, but it cannot chop vegetables. The CPU is the chef with the knife doing the chopping. No matter how big or hot the oven is, you cannot chop carrots any faster if you only have one chef with a knife. CPU-bound tokenization is the chopping phase before the GPU starts cooking.

cards.title: Tokenization in Action
cards.body: When you tokenize a massive dataset of Wikipedia articles, your CPU usage spikes to 100% while your expensive GPU sits completely idle. If you double the length of your text documents, the CPU takes exactly twice as long to process them, regardless of what GPU you have installed.

cards.title: The GPU Magic Myth
cards.body: Many developers think upgrading their GPU will automatically speed up every step of their AI pipeline. But because tokenization relies on string splitting and dictionary searches, a better GPU won't help at all. You need more CPU threads or a faster CPU clock speed!

cards.title: Division of Labor
cards.body: See how different computer parts handle different tasks during AI training.

right.text: Multiplies massive matrices of numbers for the neural network.

left.text: Splits text strings and searches vocabulary dictionaries.

exercises.prompt: Which factors actually limit how fast your text can be tokenized?

options.text: The amount of VRAM on your graphics card
The number of CPU threads and the length of the strings
The speed of your internet connection
The size of the neural network layers

exercises.explain: Tokenization speed depends on the CPU's ability to process text (string length) and how many tasks it can run at once (thread count).

exercises.prompt: True or False: Upgrading to a larger, faster GPU will speed up your tokenization process.
exercises.explain: Tokenization is a CPU-bound task that involves string manipulation and dictionary lookups, so upgrading the GPU will not make it faster.

exercises.prompt: Fill in the missing word to complete the sentence.
exercises.sentence: Because string splitting and dictionary lookups do not use matrix math, tokenization is a _ task.
exercises.explain: Splitting text and looking up words in a dictionary are logical operations handled by the CPU, not the mathematical GPU.

exercises.prompt: Which AI system correctly plans to optimize a tokenization pipeline?

options.text: AI System A: 'To speed up tokenization, we should upload the text directly to the GPU memory so the graphics card can split the strings.'
AI System B: 'We should use multi-threading on our CPU because tokenization is bound by CPU processing, not GPU power.'
AI System C: 'Tokenization speed is completely determined by how fast the SSD can read the text files.'

exercises.explain: AI System B correctly identifies that string processing is a CPU-bound task, not a GPU task.

exercises.prompt: Your AI model training is paused because the CPU is struggling to split text into tokens fast enough. How can you speed up this CPU-bound step?

options.text: Buy a GPU with double the memory (VRAM)
Increase the number of CPU worker threads
Increase the number of GPU Tensor Cores

exercises.explain: Tokenization happens entirely on the CPU because it involves text manipulation and lookup tables. To speed it up, you need a faster CPU or more CPU worker threads, not a bigger GPU.
